comments.comment_body: Congratulations Edwin!! Exciting to see you to take on this role!
comments.post_body:
I'm thrilled to share that I've taken on a new role as Director of the Boston Microsoft Technology Center (MTC). Big thanks to Merrill Schebaum for this opportunity, and a huge thanks to Sedrick Perry, Nils Oulundsen, Mike Harris, and Ben Grimes – your support and leadership over the past 2 years have been inspiring!
Our MTC Architects are ready to showcase the #artofthepossible and turn it into reality by helping your organization craft solutions that drive your business goals. You can learn more at https://lnkd.in/eTWJaD_u. Ready to dive in? Book your session today! #microsoftlife
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to an announcement about a new role as Director of the Boston Microsoft Technology Center, where the author expresses gratitude and excitement for the opportunity. The commenter is likely congratulating the author on their new position and potentially inquiring about the center's services.

comments.post_body:
Thrilled to share that I’ve started a new role at Intuit as a Product Manager on the AI Platform (GenOS) team!

This transition comes after a long and humbling interview journey, where I explored multiple opportunities and learned a lot about resilience, and staying true to what I value in a role. I’m grateful that this process helped me grow and ultimately led me to a team and mission that truly resonates with me.

At Intuit, I’ll be leading Model Serving and Model Performance Optimization efforts that help power reliable, scalable AI experiences.

I’m deeply thankful to everyone who encouraged me, shared advice, or simply checked in along the way. Your support made a real difference.

Excited for this next chapter and the chance to help build AI products that create meaningful impact. 🤍
comments.context_summary: The commenter is congratulating the author on their new role as a Product Manager at Intuit, where they will be leading AI platform efforts. The author is excited to start this new chapter and is grateful for the support they received during their interview journey.

comments.post_body:
This past Friday was my last day at Microsoft. I’m so thankful for those who helped and guided me during my amazing 4.5 years there. Mwanza Broussard, Angie Wilson , Andy Reichard , Rachel Lowinger (Zommick), Mehrnoosh Sameki, Anjani Lavania, Peicheng Shi, Fabiola M., John Brown, Melissa Adams and so many others, thank you for making my time at Microsoft so rewarding.  

I’m excited to be moving to SentinelOne as a TPM where I can continue to grow and learn with amazing people doing amazing work.
comments.context_summary: The commenter is congratulating the post author on their new role at SentinelOne after leaving Microsoft, and thanking them for sharing their gratitude towards their former colleagues. The post author is expressing their appreciation for their time at Microsoft and excitement for their new opportunity at SentinelOne.

comments.post_body:
I’m super excited to share more details about what I’ve been working on for the past two years. 

After leaving Microsoft, I co-founded Alpen Labs with three of my friends from MIT in 2022.

Initially, our goal was to bring the world closer together by making it easy for people to transact across borders. In this journey, we soon realized that the infrastructure that underlies the current financial system is unsuitable for the needs of the modern interconnected world. Hence, we decided to focus on building a more robust financial infrastructure.

Learn more about our vision: https://lnkd.in/efSCszdp
Join our team and help us build the future: bit.ly/alpen-careers
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to a post about the co-founder of Alpen Labs sharing details about their two-year journey, including their goal to improve financial infrastructure for a modern interconnected world. The post discusses the company's vision and invites readers to learn more and join their team.

comments.comment_body: Thank you for attending the session. I’m glad you found is useful! Looking forward to seeing what you build in the future!
comments.post_body:
Spent this past weekend attending The AI Summit Series at New York. As a student, I found the experience super rewarding!

A few experiences to reflect on:
Janhavi Mahajan and Nitya Narasimhan, PhD - your walkthroughs on building multimodal RAG solutions and Microsoft Azure AI Foundry were very insightful and I'm looking forward to use it for my upcoming coursework and projects.
As a new follower just getting into F1, experiencing a sim race game was exhilarating and moreover, Scuderia Ferrari HP’s collab with IBM took it to the next level. Mingxuan Z., thanks for talking to me about ongoing research at IBM and challenges faced during deployments. It’s cool that IBM's WatsonAI is using LLMs to generate AI-written recaps and strategy analysis powering their official F1 app! 
Having worked on AuraDB and Bloom previously, it was exciting to see newer GenAI features like GraphRAG at Neo4j. I’m already thinking about how I could combine graph dbs and AI for better clinical contextual retrieval and managing of complex data relationships!

Reflecting on these two days, my main takeaway was that Effective AI adoption is less about the model and more about the integration. The most impactful solutions aligned with existing business processes and catered to solving enterprise automations.

Happy to have created new connections with amazing people in AI enterprises. I'm leaving the summit energized and ready to apply this knowledge to my work!

#AISummitNY #ArtificialIntelligence #AIInnovation #AgenticAI #DataDriven  #Learning  #TechEvents  #WomenInTech
comments.context_summary: The commenter is reflecting on their experience attending The AI Summit Series in New York, where they gained insights from various speakers and exhibitors, and is excited to apply the knowledge they gained to their work, with a key takeaway that effective AI adoption is about integration with existing business processes. They found the event super rewarding and are looking forward to leveraging the new connections and learnings in their future projects.

comments.comment_body: It was great working with you. Best of luck!!
comments.post_body:
Today is my last day at Microsoft - I leave grateful for the people I met along the way and for the breadth of work I've had the privilege of contributing to. Can't think of many places where you can go from working on Windows to Azure to the forefront of AI evals in the span of 5.5 years

That said, one door closes, another opens - I'm very excited to be starting at Axon at the end of the month, and getting a chance to work with a deeply values driven team on life-saving products.
comments.context_summary: The commenter is bidding farewell to Microsoft after 5.5 years, expressing gratitude for the experiences and opportunities they had, and is now moving on to a new role at Axon. They are excited to join a values-driven team and work on life-saving products at their new company.

comments.post_body:
ATTN NEW GRADS: Are You graduating this month or next month with a BS or MS degree in Computer Science? VMware has a New Grad Opportunity - New Graduate Application Developer - in Palo Alto :
Solid programming experience working with Java frameworks like Spring, Spring Boot, Hibernate, JPA.
Experience in web application development using REST, RDMBS like Postgresql is a must.
Experience in version control systems such as Perforce, GIT

Feel free to apply via our career site. 

https://lnkd.in/e3GDFFN
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to a job posting for a New Graduate Application Developer position at VMware, which requires experience with Java frameworks and web application development. The position is open to recent graduates with a degree in Computer Science.
